fields.description:
Add a first-class ticket status `idea` for future work that is captured but not
yet designed or ready for implementation. It maps to a Jira status `IDEA` and is
deliberately kept out of the work-dispatch and store-health hot paths so a noisy,
high-volume backlog of ideas never taxes or pollutes the day-to-day parallel-agent
workflow.

## Context

Teams need a durable parking lot for rough, undesigned future work ‚Äî separate from
`open` (which means "designed enough to work; eligible for `ready`/`next-batch`").
Today the only pre-work status is `open`, so a captured idea immediately becomes
claimable work. `idea` fills that gap. It is a *status* (any ticket type can hold
it), reached by the same free transitions as every other status ‚Äî rebar gives
clients tools to follow their own workflow, not a rigid state machine. The status
is intentionally carved out of the automated dispatch surfaces (`ready`,
`next-batch`) and the completion-attestation + store-health machinery wherever its
undesigned looseness would generate noise, while remaining fully listable and
searchable so ideas can be found and promoted later.

## What / Scope

Design decisions settled in brainstorm (all confirmed):

- **First-class, free-transition status.** Add `idea` to the `TicketStatus`
  literal (`src/rebar/types.py`), the transition validator
  (`_VALID_STATUSES` in `_commands/transition.py`), and the accepted `list --status`
  set (`_engine_support/reads_cli.py`) so `list --status=idea` works. No transition
  is prohibited; `idea` can be reached from and left to any status.

- **Excluded from `ready` / `next-batch` for free.** Achieved by NOT adding `idea`
  to the "open-ish" sets (`graph/_ready.py::_OPEN_STATUSES`, the
  `status="open,in_progress"` candidate filter in `_engine_support/next_batch.py`).
  No new filter code.

- **`idea ‚Üí closed` is a reject/drop that bypasses all four completion/attestation
  close gates**, keyed on **from-status == `idea`**: completion verifier + the
  file-impact‚Üíreferencing-commit precheck (both via an early return in
  `transition_close.py::_completion_precheck`), and the bug-close-reason guard +
  story/epic signature gate (both skipped in `_commands/txn.py::transition_core`).
  The **structural open-children guard is KEPT** (integrity, not completion ‚Äî reject
  the tree bottom-up).

- **`validate` noise exemptions (Posture B, scoped).** Exempt `idea` from the checks
  that punish undesigned/loose work: `check_empty_epics` (idea-epics are empty by
  design ‚Äî the critical one), `check_missing_descriptions`, `check_interface_contracts`,
  `check_orphaned_tasks` (+ its creation-hour cluster), and `check_ticket_count`
  (parking-lot ‚âÝ active load). KEEP the structural checks for `idea`:
  `check_child_parent_deps`, `check_cross_epic_child_deps`, `check_duplicate_titles`.
  `check_in_progress_without_notes` is naturally exempt (status-gated to in_progress).
  Per-ticket gates (`clarity_check`/`check_ac`) are NOT exempt (advisory; correctly
  report "not dispatchable"). `idea` is NOT hidden from default `list`.

- **Jira mapping `idea ‚Üî IDEA` (injective; no annotation label needed).** Update the
  five hand-maintained maps: `config.local_to_jira_status`, `config.jira_to_local_status`,
  `inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` (parity-locked with the former),
  `jira_fields._LOCAL_STATUS_TO_JIRA`, `outbound_fields._LOCAL_TO_JIRA_STATUS`; add the
  parity-test assertion. `IDEA` is non-terminal (no terminal-set change). **Known Jira
  quirk (documented, not code-fixed):** `create` drops status, so a newly-synced idea
  is born in the Jira project's default status and converges to `IDEA` on a later
  reconcile pass ‚Äî and only if the Jira workflow has a transition into `IDEA`. This is
  an operator/Jira-admin prerequisite to document.

- **Atomic `idea` command (single genesis CREATE).** New command that creates an
  `epic` in status `idea` in ONE CREATE event (race-free ‚Äî no momentary-`open`
  window, mirroring `claim`'s atomicity). Accepts a title and `--description` only
  (intentionally light: no `--parent`, no `--type`). Parity across all three surfaces:
  CLI `rebar idea "<title>"`, MCP write tool `create_idea`, library `rebar.idea(...)`.
  Implemented by having `reducer/_processors.py::process_create` honor an optional
  `status` on the CREATE event (completing the one CREATE field it does not currently
  read; default stays `open`), with the `idea` command as the SOLE producer (always
  `idea`) ‚Äî no general `create --status`, so no create-in-closed backdoor.

- **Suppress the create-time "non-bug ticket lacks file_impact" stderr warning** for
  idea-created epics (noise for undesigned work).

Out of scope (separate `discovered_from` follow-up): the reconciler transition-failure
isolation bug ‚Äî a failed Jira transition should apply every other mutation in the pass
and report the failure at the END of the pass rather than aborting mid-pass. Independent
of `idea`.

## Success Criteria

- An idea can be captured in one atomic operation on any surface (CLI/MCP/lib) and is
  never momentarily claimable.
- `idea` tickets never appear in `ready` or `next-batch`, and never drag the `validate`
  health score via empty-epic/orphan/missing-description/contract/count findings, while
  still appearing in `list` and `search`.
- Rejecting an idea (`idea ‚Üí closed`) succeeds without any completion/attestation gate,
  while the structural open-children guard still holds.
- `idea` round-trips to Jira `IDEA` (subject to the documented workflow-transition
  prerequisite), with all status maps consistent and parity-tested.
- All existing gates/tests pass; the module-size and lint gates are respected.

## Acceptance Criteria

Each item is independently verifiable; no item bundles multiple deliverables.

Status vocabulary:
- [ ] `idea` is a member of the `TicketStatus` literal in `src/rebar/types.py`.
- [ ] `idea` is accepted by the transition validator (`_VALID_STATUSES`), so
      `transition <id> open idea` succeeds (exit 0).
- [ ] `transition <id> idea open` succeeds (exit 0), demonstrating a legal exit edge.
- [ ] `rebar list --status=idea` returns tickets in status `idea` and excludes others.

Dispatch exclusion:
- [ ] `rebar ready` never returns a ticket in status `idea` (test-verified).
- [ ] `rebar next-batch <epic>` never returns a ticket in status `idea` (test-verified).

`idea ‚Üí closed` gate bypass (keyed on from-status == `idea`):
- [ ] Closing from `idea` does not invoke the completion verifier (test-verified).
- [ ] Closing from `idea` skips the file-impact‚Üíreferencing-commit precheck (test-verified).
- [ ] Closing an `idea` bug does not require a `Fixed:`/`Escalated:` close reason.
- [ ] Closing an `idea` story/epic does not require a certified signature under the
      signature gate.
- [ ] Closing an `idea` parent that has non-closed children is still blocked by the
      structural open-children guard (test-verified).

`validate` exemptions (Posture B, scoped):
- [ ] `validate` emits no `check_empty_epics` finding for an `idea` epic.
- [ ] `validate` emits no `check_missing_descriptions` finding for an `idea` ticket.
- [ ] `validate` emits no `check_interface_contracts` finding for an `idea` ticket.
- [ ] `validate` emits no `check_orphaned_tasks` finding (or creation-cluster finding)
      for `idea` tickets.
- [ ] `validate` excludes `idea` tickets from `check_ticket_count`.
- [ ] `validate` still emits `check_child_parent_deps` findings for `idea` tickets when
      applicable (test-verified).
- [ ] `validate` still emits `check_cross_epic_child_deps` findings for `idea` tickets
      when applicable (test-verified).
- [ ] `validate` still emits `check_duplicate_titles` findings involving `idea` tickets.

Jira mapping (`idea ‚Üî IDEA`):
- [ ] `config.local_to_jira_status` maps `idea ‚Üí "IDEA"`.
- [ ] `config.jira_to_local_status` maps `"IDEA" ‚Üí idea`.
- [ ] `inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` maps `"IDEA" ‚Üí idea` and the parity test
      asserts equality with `config.jira_to_local_status`.
- [ ] `jira_fields._LOCAL_STATUS_TO_JIRA` maps `idea ‚Üí "IDEA"`.
- [ ] `outbound_fields._LOCAL_TO_JIRA_STATUS` maps `idea ‚Üí "IDEA"`.
- [ ] Outbound and inbound status translation round-trips `idea ‚Üî IDEA` (test-verified).

Atomic `idea` command (one CREATE event, born in `idea`, type `epic`):
- [ ] `rebar idea "<title>"` creates an `epic` in status `idea` via a single CREATE
      event, with no intermediate `open` state (test inspects the event log).
- [ ] `rebar idea "<title>" --description=‚Ä¶` records the supplied description.
- [ ] The MCP `create_idea` write tool creates an `epic` in status `idea` in one CREATE
      event.
- [ ] The library `rebar.idea(...)` creates an `epic` in status `idea` in one CREATE
      event.
- [ ] `process_create` honors an optional `status` on the CREATE event, defaulting to
      `open` when absent.
- [ ] No general `create --status` flag is added on any surface (the `idea` command is
      the sole producer of a non-`open` genesis status).

Ergonomics + docs:
- [ ] The create-time "non-bug ticket lacks file_impact" stderr warning does not fire
      for idea-created epics.
- [ ] The committed `CLAUDE.md` documents the `idea` status and its lifecycle behavior.
- [ ] `docs/event-schema.md` documents that a CREATE event may carry `status`.
- [ ] The reconciler/Jira docs document the `IDEA` workflow-transition prerequisite and
      the create-drops-status convergence quirk.
- [ ] Command/tool reference docs list `rebar idea` / `create_idea` / `rebar.idea`.

Gates:
- [ ] `make lint` passes (ruff check + format-check).
- [ ] The full test suite passes.
- [ ] No new source file exceeds the module-size soft cap without an allowlist entry.
